mnemonics: CPU Time = I * CPI * T (Imagine Clock-Pushing Time)
keyPoints: CPU Time (Execution Time) is the product of Instruction Count (I), Cycles Per Instruction (CPI), and Clock Cycle Time (T).
Improving performance means decreasing the value of any one of the three factors in the equation.
The equation highlights the trade-offs between instruction count, clock speed, and the complexity of instructions.
commonMistakes: Confusing clock cycle time (T) with clock rate (frequency); performance is inversely proportional to T but directly proportional to clock rate.
Assuming a lower CPI is always better; a lower CPI for a complex instruction set might result in a higher overall instruction count (I).
Forgetting that optimizing for one factor (e.g., decreasing I) might negatively impact another (e.g., increasing CPI or T).
